Class BudgetCycle

java.lang.Object
Models.BudgetCycle
public class BudgetCycle extends Object
Represents a single budget cycle for a user.

A budget cycle defines a period (start date to end date) during which the user tracks their spending against a total allowance. It provides methods for calculating daily limits, remaining balances, and spending percentages.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    applyRollover()
    Applies a rollover by adding the remaining balance to the total allowance and recalculating the daily limit.
    double

    Calculates the safe daily spending limit based on the total allowance and cycle duration.

    Calculates the remaining balance after summing all provided expenses.
    int

    Calculates the total number of days in this budget cycle (inclusive).

    Returns the unique identifier of this budget cycle.

    Returns the end date of this budget cycle.

    Returns the current remaining balance for this cycle.

    Calculates the percentage of the total allowance that has been spent.

    Returns the start date of this budget cycle.
    double
    getTotalAllowance()
    Returns the total allowance (budget) for this cycle.
    String
    getUserPin()
    Returns the PIN of the user who owns this cycle.
    boolean
    isEightyPercentReached()
    Checks whether 80% or more of the total allowance has been spent.
    void
    resetCycle()
    Resets the cycle by zeroing out the allowance, remaining balance, and daily limit.

    Sets the start date of this budget cycle.
    void

    Sets the total allowance for this cycle.
    void

    Sets the PIN of the user who owns this cycle.

  • Constructor Details

    • BudgetCycle

      public BudgetCycle()
      Creates an empty budget cycle instance.

  • Method Details

    • getCycleId

      public int getCycleId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the cycle ID

    • setCycleId

      public void setCycleId(int cycleId)
      Sets the unique identifier of this budget cycle.
      Parameters:
      cycleId - the cycle ID to set

    • getUserPin

      public String getUserPin()
      Returns the PIN of the user who owns this cycle.
      Returns:
      the user's PIN string

    • setUserPin

      public void setUserPin(String userPin)
      Sets the PIN of the user who owns this cycle.
      Parameters:
      userPin - the user PIN to associate with this cycle

    • getTotalAllowance

      public double getTotalAllowance()
      Returns the total allowance (budget) for this cycle.
      Returns:
      the total allowance amount

    • setTotalAllowance

      public void setTotalAllowance(double totalAllowance)
      Sets the total allowance for this cycle.
      Parameters:
      totalAllowance - the allowance amount (must be positive)

    • getStartDate

      public Date getStartDate()
      Returns the start date of this budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the start date

    • setStartDate

      public void setStartDate(Date startDate)
      Sets the start date of this budget cycle.
      Parameters:
      startDate - the start date to set

    • getEndDate

      public Date getEndDate()
      Returns the end date of this budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the end date

    • setEndDate

      public void setEndDate(Date endDate)
      Sets the end date of this budget cycle.
      Parameters:
      endDate - the end date to set

    • getRemainingBalance

      public double getRemainingBalance()
      Returns the current remaining balance for this cycle.
      Returns:
      the remaining balance

    • calculateTotalDays

      public int calculateTotalDays()
      Calculates the total number of days in this budget cycle (inclusive).
      Returns:
      the number of days between start and end date, or 0 if dates are not set

    • calculateDailyLimit

      public double calculateDailyLimit()
      Calculates the safe daily spending limit based on the total allowance and cycle duration.

      Also updates the internal safeDailyLimit field.

      Returns:
      the daily limit amount, or 0 if the cycle has no valid days

    • calculateRemainingBalance

      public double calculateRemainingBalance(List<Expense> expenses)
      Calculates the remaining balance after summing all provided expenses.

      Also updates the internal remainingBalance field.

      Parameters:
      expenses - the list of expenses to deduct from the allowance; may be null
      Returns:
      the remaining balance after all expenses are deducted

    • getSpentPercentage

      public double getSpentPercentage()
      Calculates the percentage of the total allowance that has been spent.
      Returns:
      the spent percentage (0–100), or 0.0 if allowance is not set

    • isEightyPercentReached

      public boolean isEightyPercentReached()
      Checks whether 80% or more of the total allowance has been spent.
      Returns:
      true if spending has reached or exceeded 80%; false otherwise

    • applyRollover

      public void applyRollover()
      Applies a rollover by adding the remaining balance to the total allowance and recalculating the daily limit.

    • resetCycle

      public void resetCycle()
      Resets the cycle by zeroing out the allowance, remaining balance, and daily limit.
